title: rhus radicans var. vulgaris
intro: Rhus radicans var. vulgaris, also known as poison ivy, is a plant species native to North America, characterized by its shiny leaves and red stems. The plant contains urushiol, a potent allergenic oil, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The root of the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema, and has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Rhus radicans var. vulgaris include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, skin irritations, and other health issues.
